name: Eggnog Cake/cw
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Country Woman, Nov/Dec, 1995
Cream butter and 3/4 cup sugar until smooth. Add yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition. Mix together orange juice, orange peel, and vanilla. In a separate bowl, combine dry ingredients (flour, baking powder, nutmeg, baking soda, salt) with the remaining sugar. Alternately add these dry ingredients and orange juice mixture to the butter-sugar mixture, beating well after each addition. Beat egg whites until foamy; gradually incorporate remaining sugar until soft peaks form. Gently fold the white mixture into the batter. Grease two 9-inch round cake pans with wax paper and lining them. Pour batter evenly into prepared pans. Bake at 350°F for 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Allow to cool for 5 minutes before removing from oven, then transfer cakes onto wire racks. Peel off the wax paper and allow to cool completely.
For filling: In a small saucepan, combine flour with a small amount of eggnog. Stir until smooth. Gradually add remaining eggnog while stirring constantly over low heat until it boils for 2 minutes before turning off heat. Allow to cool completely. For frosting, melt butter and stir in vanilla, nutmeg, and powdered sugar until well combined. Beat in whipped cream until light and fluffy. Slowly incorporate hot water until the frosting reaches a slightly runny consistency.
Split cakes into horizontal layers; spread filling on three of the six layers. Stack the remaining plain layer on top and frost the entire cake with the chocolate frosting.
